personal.name: Ishan Paharia
personal.headline: I architect resilient backend systems and pair them with clean, functional frontends.

about: I'm a final-year Computer Science student at The LNM Institute of Information Technology, Jaipur (2023 - 2027), with a strong focus on backend engineering and systems design, and a growing comfort with shipping polished frontends to match.

My work centers on building resilient, real-time systems: horizontally-scaled WebSocket infrastructure, Redis-backed caching pipelines, and transactional database flows that stay consistent under concurrent load.

I've shipped full-stack products end-to-end, from Pixnette, a real-time collaborative pixel canvas, to CoursessionAI, an AI-powered platform that turns YouTube playlists into structured courses with quizzes and a context-aware tutor. Outside of building, I spend a lot of time on competitive programming.

projects.title: Pixnette
projects.description: Real-time collaborative pixel canvas with horizontally-scaled WebSocket infrastructure.
details: Engineered horizontal scaling across 2 Node.js instances behind an Nginx round-robin reverse proxy, sustaining 300 concurrent WebSocket connections on a self-hosted VPS.

Built a Redis write-back pipeline with an ordered event queue, atomic RENAME flush locking, and timestamp-guarded UPSERTs to guarantee canvas consistency and full timelapse history across bulk PostgreSQL flushes.
Synchronized multi-instance Socket.io state via the @socket.io/redis-adapter Pub/Sub and a binary Redis canvas buffer using SETRANGE/GET for O(1) per-pixel read/write.
Hardened deployment with Redis TTL cooldowns, UUID/IP-UA fingerprinting, Nginx rate limits on WebSocket and API routes, and GitHub Actions CI/CD.

projects.title: CoursessionAI
projects.description: AI learning platform that converts YouTube playlists into structured courses with summaries, quizzes, and an AI tutor.
details: Built a full-stack platform that converts any YouTube playlist into a structured course with AI-generated module hierarchies, per-video summaries, quizzes, and a context-aware AI tutor.
Engineered an atomic playlist ingestion pipeline using Neon's WebSocket client for multi-step transactional DB writes, eliminating partial-state corruption across course, module, and video inserts.
Implemented layered access control, Clerk JWT verification, user-level course ownership, and resource-level integrity checks, securing all progress, notes, bookmark, and AI endpoints.
Deployed on Vercel with rate-limited APIs, Svix webhook verification via raw body capture, and XSS-sanitized completion certificate generation.

chatDatabase.question: What is your name?
chatDatabase.answer: I'm Ishan Paharia, a final-year Computer Science student at The LNM Institute of Information Technology, Jaipur, and a backend-focused full-stack developer.

chatDatabase.question: Show me your projects
chatDatabase.answer: Here are some of the systems I've built, real-time and AI-powered platforms with a strong backend foundation.

chatDatabase.question: What is your tech stack?
chatDatabase.answer: I work across the stack, with particular strength on the backend (Node.js, Express, Django, FastAPI, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis), plus React and Next.js on the frontend.

chatDatabase.question: Tell me about your experience
chatDatabase.answer: I'm a final-year student, so my experience comes from building real-world apps and competing in coding contests rather than full-time roles, check out my projects!

chatDatabase.question: How can I contact you?
chatDatabase.answer: You can reach out via email or connect with me on GitHub and LinkedIn, click any button below!

chatDatabase.question: What are your current interests?
chatDatabase.answer: Outside of building, I spend a lot of time on competitive programming and tinkering on hobby projects, here's what I'm focused on.

chatDatabase.question: What are your competitive programming achievements?
chatDatabase.answer: I have achieved a max rating of 1465 (Specialist) on Codeforces (current rating 1336), 1746 (3-Star Coder) on CodeChef, and a rating of 1853 (Knight) on LeetCode with 200+ problems solved.
